title: ML/AI with Tradingview-mcp
author: kidonabike
selftext:
Wondering if anyone is having success in similar endeavors?

  
I've built a data pipeline using tradingview-mcp that receives a bunch of indicator data including some htf data, and ohlcv data.

I've built a python/qwen bot that will for the purpose of optimization simulate taking every trade from my current buy/sell alert settings, and log rich datasets while simulating these trades.

I will then use ML algorithms to determine the best combinations of indicator data both at and before the buy/sell alerts, and use that to build the filtering system into my algo so that it learns how to discern between a valid or invalidated alert!

Does this sound crazy? It's all currently working, going to collect sim data for a week or two before looking at optimizing.

My strategy uses a custom set of indicators + htf data to scalp/intraday the 5m chart.

comments.author: Mu1tikus
comments.body: Hello. Please tell us about the results. I'm interested to know what happens.

comments.author: d_e_g_m
comments.body: does TV has an api so we can read OLCH data, or just went to the web and scrapped it?

replies.author: kidonabike
replies.body: Im using an indicator alert to send ohlcv data out to a webhook every candle

replies.author: d_e_g_m
replies.body: Wow... what ingeniosity. Doesn't your broker provide free market data?

replies.author: kidonabike
replies.body: Not easily for futures where I live sadly.

comments.author: Intelligent-Log191
comments.body:
Not crazy. Just be careful not to train the filter on the same noisy alerts it is supposed to judge.

Log three things separately: valid alert that worked, valid alert that failed, and forced/invalid alert you should have skipped. Add fees, slippage and regime tags before optimizing.

That's basically the split we use in TradingWizard: structured setup first (entry/stop/target/confidence), then paper/bot behavior after. If those layers blur, the model starts curve-fitting your hope.

replies.author: kidonabike
replies.body: This is very helpful thank you.

comments.author: PleasantSomewhere990
comments.body: Honestly the hard part is avoiding overfit once the sim data starts looking good
